tactical_shape: Second-half positional tweak with Michael Olise moved more centrally to improve connection and create more danger.
High-intensity, technically strong, dominant after the break; more direct attacking play through central combinations and through balls.
standout_players: Kylian Mbappé — ruthless efficient, scored twice, including the winner and a record-breaking 58th France goal.
Michael Olise — superlative playmaking; the more he touched the ball, the better; central role created more danger.
Mike Maignan — made a good save from Sadio Mané in the first half.
Bradley Barcola — off the bench and scored the third.
Adrien Rabiot — assertive play leading to Barcola’s goal.
home_read.momentum: A statement win for the World Cup favourites; despite a poor first half, France’s second-half quality and Mbappé’s record give them strong momentum.

tactical_shape: Keenly fought contest in the first half, with Senegal threatening on the break and in attack before France took control.
Looked dangerous enough to lead at half-time had chances been taken, but were then exposed by losing the ball cheaply and France’s technical quality.
standout_players: Sadio Mané — had a good first-half chance saved by Maignan.
Ismaïla Sarr — wasted a clear opportunity from 10 yards.
Ibrahim Mbaye — as a Senegal substitute, scored with a rising drive in added time.
Édouard Mendy — beaten twice by Mbappé; should have dealt better with the second.
away_read.momentum: A warning about efficiency and level-hitting, but not fatal; Senegal still have two matches left and qualification remains possible.
